questions.context:
[Situation: two classmates discussing a group project deadline]
Speaker: "Why are we redoing the whole slide deck again?"
questions.stem: Why are we redoing the whole slide deck again?

options.A: Beats me — nobody told me either.
options.B: The library closes at nine tonight.
options.D: Sometime after the meeting, I think.

questions.context:
[Situation: student asking about a reading list for a course]
Speaker: "I still can't find the reading list anywhere."
questions.stem: I still can't find the reading list anywhere.

options.A: That novel was a great read.
options.B: Actually, it's pinned on the course website.
options.C: The cafeteria stops serving at eight.
options.D: No, I haven't returned it yet.

questions.context:
[Situation: checking if a service is open]
Speaker: "How late's the post office open today?"
questions.stem: How late's the post office open today?

options.B: I need to mail a package.
options.C: Well, they usually close around five.

questions.context:
[Situation: asking a neighbor about noise]
Speaker: "Didn't the drilling next door keep you up?"
questions.stem: Didn't the drilling next door keep you up?

options.A: The recycling goes out on Tuesdays.
options.B: They're putting up a new fence.
options.C: It's coming from the third floor.
options.D: Honestly, I slept right through it.

questions.context:
[Situation: student checking with a classmate about lab report requirements]
Speaker: "Where am I supposed to submit the lab report?"
questions.stem: Where am I supposed to submit the lab report?

options.A: Your guess is as good as mine.

questions.context:
[Situation: calling to check business hours]
Speaker: "Aren't you open for another hour or so?"
questions.stem: Aren't you open for another hour or so?

options.B: Actually, we're closing in ten minutes.
options.C: The parking lot is being repaved.

questions.context:
[Situation: student discussing a presentation topic with a partner]
Speaker: "What's a good topic for our presentation?"
questions.stem: What's a good topic for our presentation?

options.A: The projector in that room is broken.
options.B: I finally returned those library books.
options.C: How about renewable energy in cities?

questions.context:
[Situation: asking for directions to a nearby location]
Speaker: "I'm trying to find the nearest pharmacy."
questions.stem: I'm trying to find the nearest pharmacy.

options.A: The weather turned cold this morning.
options.B: I take that same medicine too.
options.D: Are you okay with a short walk?

questions.context:
[Situation: student asking a classmate about a midterm exam]
Speaker: "When's the midterm actually starting on Monday?"
questions.stem: When's the midterm actually starting on Monday?

options.A: The exam covers the first five chapters.
options.B: Well, the email said nine sharp.
options.C: My roommate adopted a kitten yesterday.
options.D: In the big lecture hall, I think.

questions.context:
[Situation: someone struggling to open a jar in the kitchen]
Speaker: "I can't get this jar to open."
questions.stem: I can't get this jar to open.

options.A: Here, let me give it a try.
options.C: The bus was late again today.
